label: Evolutionary Economics
note: Veblen, Nelson & Winter — variation/selection/retention in firms.

label: Memetics (Dawkins)
note: Selfish Gene chapter 11; later expanded by Blackmore.

label: Evolutionary Computation / Neuroevolution
note: GA, ES, NEAT — selection over parameter populations.

label: Shannon Entropy
note: Shannon explicitly borrowed Boltzmann's H.

label: Compartmental epidemic models
note: Same coupled ODE structure as Lotka-Volterra.

label: Bayesian Brain / Predictive Processing
note: Friston's FEP is Bayesian inference in neural substrate.

label: Connectionist learning rules
note: Both update synaptic weights from local signals.

label: Punctuated paradigm shifts
note: Eldredge/Gould and Kuhn often analogized.

label: Monetary policy as control system
note: Taylor rule = PID-like controller.

label: Evolutionary game theory
note: Maynard Smith — ESS solves IPD.

label: Scale-free networks
note: Barabási-Albert — preferential attachment generates power laws.

label: RL as formal behaviorism
note: Sutton & Barto explicitly draw on Skinner / Thorndike.

label: Clonal selection theory
note: Burnet's clonal selection — Darwinism inside the body.

label: Bass diffusion / viral adoption
note: Bass model maps SIR onto product adoption.

label: Threshold contagion models
note: Granovetter — threshold models of collective behavior.

label: Sandpile finance / critical markets
note: Sornette, Bak — markets near critical points.

label: Variational principles in brain
note: Friston frames FEP as a least-action style principle.

label: Variational Bayes
note: VI is a tractable approximation to Bayesian posterior.

label: Industrial organization as repeated games
note: Tirole et al — oligopoly as IPD.

label: Field theory in sociology
note: Bourdieu took the 'field' metaphor explicitly from physics.

label: Pareto wealth distributions
note: Wealth follows power laws under multiplicative accumulation.

label: Brownian motion / Einstein 1905
note: Diffusion is the macroscopic limit of random walks.

label: Turing morphogenesis
note: Turing 1952 — reaction-diffusion explains pattern formation.

label: Three-factor learning rules
note: Neuromodulated Hebbian = biologically plausible RL.

label: Predictive coding under FEP
note: FEP subsumes predictive coding as a corollary.

label: Ant colony optimization
note: Dorigo — stigmergic optimization.

label: Pure-drift training
note: Training nets via undirected random perturbation alone doesn't scale; needs gradient or population selection.

label: Karma-as-credit-assignment
note: Sometimes drawn as analogy in pop-AI writing; not a rigorous research program.
